features.title: Anime episode & status notifications
features.summary: Allow users to subscribe to anime and receive in-app and email notifications when new episodes air or anime status changes.
user_stories: As a user, I want to be notified when a new episode of an anime I'm watching airs so that I don't miss it
As a user, I want to receive an email digest of upcoming episodes so I can plan my watching schedule
As a user, I want to auto-subscribe to anime on my watching list so I don't have to manually follow each one
requirements: Subscribe/unsubscribe button on anime detail page
In-app notification bell with unread count in navbar
Email notifications with configurable frequency (immediate, daily, weekly)
Auto-subscribe option for anime on user's watching list
Notification preferences page in account settings
Detect new episodes via airing schedule sync comparison
Detect status changes via anime sync comparison
acceptance_criteria: User can subscribe to an anime from its detail page
When a new episode airs for a subscribed anime, user sees in-app notification within 1 hour
Email digests are sent at user's configured frequency
User can disable all notifications globally
Bulk sync operations do not spam notifications
features.technical_notes: Requires email infrastructure (Mailgun/SES on Forge), Laravel Notifications (database + mail channels), and ideally WebSockets for real-time in-app delivery (Laravel Echo + Pusher/Soketi). New tables: anime_subscriptions, notifications, notification_preferences. Hooks into SyncAiringSchedulePage and SyncAnimePage completion.
risks: Notification volume during bulk syncs could spam users
Email deliverability setup (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) adds infrastructure complexity
Real-time in-app notifications need WebSocket server

decision_log.decision: Feature proposed by user. Deferred to v1.1 — requires email infrastructure, WebSocket setup, and careful volume management. Full spec added to PROJECT_PLAN.md.

features.title: Manga tracking
features.summary: Add manga as a trackable media type alongside anime, with its own catalog, detail pages, and user list management.
user_stories: As a user, I want to track manga I'm reading alongside my anime list so I have one unified tracker
As a user, I want to browse and search manga the same way I browse anime
requirements: Manga model with AniList manga fields (chapters, volumes, status, etc.)
Manga sync pipeline from AniList (type: MANGA)
Manga detail pages with chapter/volume info
User manga list with reading statuses and chapter progress
Search includes manga results (filterable by type)
Profile stats include manga statistics
acceptance_criteria: User can browse, search, and view manga detail pages
User can add manga to their list with chapter/volume progress
Manga and anime lists are separate but accessible from the same profile
MAL XML import handles manga exports
features.technical_notes: Effectively doubles the data model. Could reuse the Anime model with a 'type' discriminator or create a separate Manga model. AniList uses the same GraphQL schema with type: MANGA. Sync pipeline can be extended with a --type flag. Major UI work for manga-specific fields (chapters vs episodes).
risks: Doubles sync time and storage requirements
UI complexity — manga has different metadata than anime (chapters, volumes, no airing schedule)
Scope creep risk — light novels, one-shots, manhwa subtypes

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Doubles the data model and UI surface area. Get anime tracking solid first.

features.title: MAL OAuth2 sync
features.summary: Two-way sync with MyAnimeList via OAuth2 API, allowing users to keep their MAL and AniTrack lists in sync automatically.
user_stories: As a user, I want to connect my MAL account so my list stays in sync without manual XML exports
As a user, I want changes I make on AniTrack to reflect on MAL automatically
requirements: MAL OAuth2 authorization flow (redirect, callback, token storage)
Pull user's MAL list and merge with local list
Push local list changes back to MAL
Conflict resolution strategy (last-write-wins or user choice)
Token refresh handling
acceptance_criteria: User can link their MAL account via OAuth
Initial sync imports full MAL list
Subsequent syncs detect and merge changes from both sides
User can disconnect MAL account
features.technical_notes: MAL OAuth2 approval can take weeks. XML import covers 90% of the use case. Two-way sync has inherent conflict resolution complexity. Laravel Socialite or manual OAuth2 flow. Need to store refresh tokens securely (encrypted in DB).
risks: MAL OAuth API approval process is slow and unpredictable
Two-way sync conflict resolution is complex
MAL API rate limits are strict (few requests/second)

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. XML import covers the primary use case. OAuth approval timeline is unpredictable.

features.title: Jikan (MAL) data integration
features.summary: Integrate Jikan v4 REST API as a secondary data source to supplement AniList with MAL-specific data like character details, reviews, and recommendations.
user_stories: As a user, I want to see MAL scores alongside AniList scores for a broader perspective
As a user, I want to see character and voice actor information on anime detail pages
requirements: Jikan API client service with rate limiting
Map MAL IDs to AniList IDs (mal_id field already stored)
Fetch and cache supplementary data (characters, staff, reviews)
Display MAL score on detail page alongside AniList score
acceptance_criteria: Anime detail page shows MAL score when available
Character/staff data loads without impacting page performance
Jikan failures don't break the page (graceful degradation)
features.technical_notes: AniList already covers 95% of data needs. Jikan is useful for characters, voice actors, and MAL community data. Rate limit: 3 req/sec, 60 req/min. Could be fetched on-demand rather than bulk synced. mal_id is already stored on the anime model.
risks: Jikan is an unofficial MAL API — could break without notice
Additional API dependency increases failure surface
Data mapping between AniList and MAL IDs isn't always 1:1

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. AniList covers 95% of data needs. Low priority supplement.

features.title: Recommendations engine
features.summary: Suggest anime to users based on their watch history, scores, and genre preferences. Could use collaborative filtering or content-based approaches.
user_stories: As a user, I want to discover new anime based on what I've enjoyed so far
As a user, I want to see 'if you liked X, you'll like Y' suggestions on anime detail pages
requirements: Algorithm to generate recommendations (content-based or collaborative filtering)
Recommendations section on anime detail page
Personalized recommendations page/section on dashboard
Ability to dismiss/hide recommendations
acceptance_criteria: User with 10+ rated anime sees relevant recommendations
Recommendations update as user adds ratings
Recommendations don't include anime already on user's list
features.technical_notes: For MVP of this feature, could start with AniList's built-in recommendations data (already in relations). True collaborative filtering needs user volume. Content-based (genre/studio similarity scoring) is feasible with current data. DB schema already planned for recommendations table.
risks: Cold start problem — needs enough user ratings to be useful
Algorithm quality directly impacts perceived value
Computational cost if doing real-time collaborative filtering

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Needs user volume for collaborative filtering. Can start with content-based similarity using existing genre/studio data.

features.title: User reviews
features.summary: Allow users to write and publish reviews for anime they've watched, with ratings and helpful/not-helpful voting.
user_stories: As a user, I want to write a review for anime I've completed to share my thoughts
As a user, I want to read other users' reviews to help decide what to watch
requirements: Review form on anime detail page (text, overall score, optional category scores)
Reviews list on anime detail page (sorted by helpfulness/recency)
One review per user per anime
Helpful/not-helpful voting on reviews
User's reviews visible on their profile
acceptance_criteria: User can write, edit, and delete their own review
Reviews display with author, date, score, and text
Reviews are sorted by helpfulness by default
User must have the anime on their list to review it
features.technical_notes: DB schema already planned for reviews table. Requires moderation tooling (report button, admin review queue) if opening to multiple users. For personal use, moderation is unnecessary. Markdown support for review text would be nice.
risks: Moderation burden if user base grows
Spam/abuse potential without moderation
Low value for personal use — mainly useful with community

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Requires moderation tooling. Limited value for personal/small use.

features.title: Forums / clubs
features.summary: Community features including discussion forums organized by anime/topic, and user-created clubs/groups.
user_stories: As a user, I want to discuss anime with other users in dedicated forums
As a user, I want to join clubs based on shared interests
requirements: Forum boards organized by category (per-anime, general, off-topic)
Thread creation, replies, quoting
Clubs with member lists, descriptions, and private discussion boards
Moderation tools (ban, mute, delete, lock threads)
acceptance_criteria: Users can create and participate in forum discussions
Forum threads are linked to relevant anime pages
Moderation tools prevent spam and abuse
features.technical_notes: Major feature requiring significant new models (boards, threads, posts, clubs, memberships), real-time updates, moderation queue, and notification integration. Completely separate domain from the core tracker. Consider if a Discord server would serve the same purpose with zero dev effort.
risks: Massive scope — effectively building a second product
Moderation burden scales with user count
Could distract from core tracker value proposition

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v2.0. Major feature with heavy moderation burden. A Discord server may serve the same purpose.

features.title: Custom list themes
features.summary: Allow users to customize the appearance of their public anime list page with custom CSS themes, colors, and layouts.
user_stories: As a user, I want to personalize my list page so it reflects my style
As a user, I want to choose from pre-made themes or create my own
requirements: Theme selector in profile settings
Pre-built theme options (5-10 starter themes)
Optional custom CSS editor for advanced users
Theme preview before applying
CSS sanitization to prevent XSS
acceptance_criteria: User can select a theme and it applies to their public list page
Custom CSS is sanitized and cannot execute scripts
Themes don't break list functionality
features.technical_notes: Security is the main concern — custom CSS can be used for XSS attacks (url(), expression(), behavior). Need a CSS sanitizer/whitelist. Safer approach: predefined theme variables (colors, fonts, spacing) rather than raw CSS. Could use CSS custom properties scoped to the list container.
risks: XSS via custom CSS (expression(), url(), background-image data URIs)
Maintenance burden of CSS sanitization
Performance impact of user-injected styles

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v2.0. Security implications require careful CSS sanitization. Low priority for personal use.

features.title: Native mobile apps
features.summary: Native iOS and Android apps for AniTrack, providing a mobile-optimized experience with push notifications and offline support.
user_stories: As a user, I want a native app so I can quickly update my list from my phone
As a user, I want push notifications for new episodes on my mobile device
requirements: iOS and Android apps (React Native, Flutter, or native)
Core features: browse, search, list management, anime detail
Push notifications for subscribed anime
Offline list viewing and queued updates
acceptance_criteria: App provides feature parity with core web functionality
Push notifications work reliably
Offline mode allows viewing list without connection
features.technical_notes: The web app must be mobile-responsive first — this validates the UX before investing in native. Could consider a PWA as an intermediate step (service worker, manifest, installable). React Native or Flutter would allow code sharing. Requires a public API (F-013).
risks: Massive development effort — effectively a second frontend
App store approval and maintenance overhead
Requires public API to exist first

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v2.0+. SPA must be mobile-responsive first. Consider PWA as intermediate step.

features.title: Email verification
features.summary: Require users to verify their email address after registration before accessing full functionality.
user_stories: As an admin, I want users to verify their email so I know accounts are legitimate
As a user, I want to recover my account via email if I forget my password
requirements: Verification email sent on registration
Verification link with signed URL and expiry
Resend verification email option
Restrict certain actions until verified (optional)
acceptance_criteria: New users receive a verification email within 1 minute
Clicking the link verifies the account
Expired links can be resent
Verified status shown in user profile
features.technical_notes: Laravel has this built-in (MustVerifyEmail contract, verification routes). Minimal code effort. Main blocker is email infrastructure — need a mail provider configured on Forge (Mailgun, SES, Postmark). Shares infra dependency with F-001 (notifications).
risks: Requires email delivery infrastructure
Could friction the registration flow for a personal use app

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Not needed for personal use. Implement when spam accounts become an issue.

features.title: SEO (meta tags, OG images, sitemap)
features.summary: Add proper SEO meta tags, Open Graph images, Twitter Cards, and a sitemap for search engine visibility and social sharing.
user_stories: As a user, I want anime pages to show a rich preview when I share links on Discord/Twitter
As an admin, I want anime pages to be indexable by search engines
requirements: Dynamic meta tags (title, description) on anime detail pages
Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags with cover images
XML sitemap generated for all anime pages
robots.txt configuration
Structured data (JSON-LD) for anime entries
acceptance_criteria: Sharing an anime link on Discord/Twitter shows title, image, and description
Google Search Console shows pages being indexed
Sitemap is accessible and auto-updates with new anime
features.technical_notes: Inertia.js supports SSR for SEO, but adds Node server complexity. Alternative: use Inertia's Head component for meta tags (works for social crawlers that execute JS). Sitemap can be generated via a scheduled artisan command. OG images could use AniList cover images directly.
risks: Inertia SPA may not be crawled well without SSR
OG image generation adds complexity if not using AniList images directly

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Personal use first. Implement when sharing publicly matters.

features.title: Monitoring (Sentry, uptime checks)
features.summary: Add error tracking (Sentry), uptime monitoring, and performance monitoring to catch issues proactively.
user_stories: As an admin, I want to be alerted when errors occur in production so I can fix them quickly
As an admin, I want to know if the site goes down before users tell me
requirements: Sentry integration for PHP and JS error tracking
Uptime monitoring with alerting (UptimeRobot, Oh Dear, or similar)
Performance monitoring (slow queries, slow requests)
Alert notifications via email or Slack
acceptance_criteria: Unhandled exceptions appear in Sentry within 1 minute
Downtime triggers an alert within 5 minutes
Slow queries (>1s) are logged and trackable
features.technical_notes: Sentry has a free tier and official Laravel SDK. Forge has built-in server monitoring. Could also use Laravel Telescope for local debugging. Quick wins: Sentry SDK install + .env config, UptimeRobot free tier for HTTP checks.
risks: Sentry free tier has event limits
Alert fatigue if thresholds are too sensitive

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.1. Add when reliability matters at scale. Quick to implement when needed.

features.title: Public API
features.summary: Expose a public REST or GraphQL API for third-party integrations, mobile apps, and browser extensions.
user_stories: As a developer, I want an API to build integrations with AniTrack
As a user, I want browser extensions or mobile apps to interact with my list
requirements: Versioned REST API (v1) with JSON responses
API authentication (API keys or OAuth2 tokens)
Rate limiting per API key
API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
Endpoints for: anime catalog, search, user list CRUD
acceptance_criteria: API docs are accessible and accurate
Third-party clients can authenticate and query anime data
Rate limiting prevents abuse
API versioning allows non-breaking evolution
features.technical_notes: Currently using Inertia.js (no separate API routes). Would need dedicated API routes in routes/api.php with Sanctum token auth. Laravel has built-in rate limiting, API Resources already exist. Could generate OpenAPI docs from code annotations.
risks: Maintenance burden of API backwards compatibility
Security surface area increases significantly
Rate limiting and abuse prevention complexity

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v2.0. Needs rate limiting, docs, versioning. No current external consumers.

features.title: i18n / multi-language support
features.summary: Internationalize the UI to support multiple languages, starting with English as default and adding community translations.
user_stories: As a non-English-speaking user, I want to use AniTrack in my native language
As an admin, I want the app to be translatable without code changes
requirements: Extract all UI strings to translation files (Laravel lang + Vue i18n)
Language switcher in UI
Persist language preference per user
RTL support for Arabic/Hebrew (if applicable)
Anime titles already have romaji/english/native — surface native titles based on language
acceptance_criteria: All UI text is translatable via language files
User can switch language and it persists across sessions
Missing translations fall back to English
features.technical_notes: Laravel has built-in localization. Vue i18n (vue-i18n) for frontend strings. Biggest effort is extracting all hardcoded strings. Anime titles already store romaji, english, and native variants — could auto-select based on user language. Community translation via Crowdin or similar.
risks: String extraction is tedious and error-prone
RTL layouts require significant CSS adjustments
Translation quality without professional translators

decision_log.decision: Deferred to v1.2. English-only for launch. Extract strings early if possible to reduce future effort.
